description_long: This pastry shop is a narrow, multi-story brick building with a prominent red facade and a steep, gabled roof. The front features three rows of rectangular windows with white trim, and a large display window at street level, suggesting a commercial storefront. A single door with a small set of steps and a metal railing provides entry, positioned to the right of the display window. The sides and rear of the building are plain and unadorned, painted in a neutral gray, with no visible windows or decorative elements. The roof is pitched and symmetrical, with a central ridge and overhanging eaves. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of urban commercial architecture.
description: A tall, red-brick pastry shop with white-trimmed windows, a large front display window, and a gabled roof, featuring a simple urban storefront design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the red facade with three rows of white-trimmed windows, a large display window, a single entry door with steps, and a gabled roof.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, gray wall with no windows or decorative features, and a small portion of the front steps and railing.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat, red wall with the gabled roof above, lacking any windows or architectural details.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing a featureless gray wall with no openings or decorations.
attribute_architectural_style: Simple urban vernacular with a gabled roof and rectangular windows, lacking ornate or period-specific details.
